pre_registration.hypothesis: FM-24 can be hardened from HIGH UNMITIGATED to MINIMAL by adding a creation-time prescription guard to check.sh
pre_registration.threshold: Guard correctly flags prescriptive lessons without enforcement path
pre_registration.falsification: Guard produces >50% false positives (flagging lessons with actual enforcement)

method: Added FM-24 guard to check.sh: scans staged lesson files for ## Rule or ## Prescription sections. If found but no tool/file reference (tools/, .py, .sh), emits NOTICE. NOTICE-level only (does not block commits). Creation-time enforcement per L-601.
actual: Guard implemented in check.sh. Tested: L-988 (## Prescription, no tool ref) correctly flagged. Guard fires only on staged files — creation-time enforcement. NOTICE level, not blocking. FM-24 now MINIMAL (1 automated defense layer).
diff: Expected N>=5 flagged in test run. Actual: 340 existing lessons would be flagged but guard only checks staged files (by design). Guard lighter-weight than enforcement_router.py — complements rather than replaces.

results.detection_method: grep ## Rule/## Prescription + absence of tool/file references
results.swiss_cheese_upgrade_path: 2nd layer needed for ADEQUATE (e.g., lesson template tool-ref requirement)
